title: Triangle classifier — GitHub proof
summary: Classify three integer side lengths as invalid, equilateral, isosceles, or scalene. The instructor suite looks complete but misses a positional misconception.

reference.title: Instructor reference

accepted_solutions.title: Accepted independent solution

misconception_programs.title: Only checks the first equal pair
misconception_programs.misconception: Isosceles means a == b, ignoring side permutations.

misconception_programs.title: Accepts degenerate triangles
misconception_programs.misconception: Uses strict inequality and accepts a + b == c.

misconception_programs.title: Labels equilateral as isosceles
misconception_programs.misconception: Checks any equality before all-three equality.

misconception_programs.title: Never checks the last equal pair
misconception_programs.misconception: Covers a=b and a=c but omits b=c.

misconception_programs.title: Only checks the last equal pair
misconception_programs.misconception: Isosceles means b == c, ignoring side permutations.

misconception_programs.title: Only checks the outer equal pair
misconception_programs.misconception: Isosceles means a == c, ignoring side permutations.

misconception_programs.title: Checks b=c only in descending order
misconception_programs.misconception: Adds an accidental a > b guard to one side permutation.

misconception_programs.title: Rejects scalene triangles
misconception_programs.misconception: Treats a valid unequal triangle as invalid.
